notes: Generated intermediate dialogs with practical travel, work, and daily-life nouns.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Mary, are you going to the hospital after work?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, I need to visit the hospital at six.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do you want me to drive the car?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, please. My bag is heavy today.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Can we stop by the school first?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Sure, the school is on the same road.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Great, then we can go straight to the hospital.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: After that, I will drop you home by car.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Thanks, Mary. That helps a lot.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: No problem. Lets leave work now.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What time is your flight to Hong Kong?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: My Hong Kong flight leaves from the airport at nine.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did you pack your bag already?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, but I still need my work laptop.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Is your car ready for the airport drive?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, I parked the car near school.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Why near school?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Mary borrowed it after work and returned it there.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Okay, lets pick it up and go to the airport.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Perfect. Please remind me to take my bag.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: How is your first week at the new school?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: The school is busy, but I like it.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do you go to work by car?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Most days, yes. My car saves time.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did Mary start work there too?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, Mary works in the school office.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Can you meet after work at the hospital cafe?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Sure, I need to visit a friend in the hospital.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Bring your bag. I have your airport documents.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Thanks, I need them for my Hong Kong trip.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Where is Mary today?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Mary is at work near the airport.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Is she driving the car or taking a bus?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: She took the car because her bag is full.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What is in the bag?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: School papers and hospital forms.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That sounds like a long day.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, she has school meetings after work.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Will she still travel to Hong Kong tomorrow?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, she will go to the airport at dawn.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Can you help me plan tomorrow?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Sure. I start work at eight.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: I need to take Mary to the hospital first.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Then we can drive to school by car.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: After school, I need to go to the airport.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Is this for your Hong Kong meeting?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes, and my bag is not packed yet.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Pack your bag tonight after work.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Good idea. Can you call me at seven?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Of course. I will call before school.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did you see my car keys?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: They are next to your bag on the chair.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Thanks. I must drive Mary to work.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Then are you going to school?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Yes, school starts in thirty minutes.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Dont forget the hospital report.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Right, it is in my bag.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: And your airport ticket to Hong Kong?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Also in my bag. I checked twice.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Great. Your car trip should be smooth.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Why are you late for work today?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: The road to school was blocked.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did you still reach the hospital on time?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, Mary waited with my bag at the gate.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That was helpful.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Very. Then we drove the car to the airport.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Was the airport crowded?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, many flights to Hong Kong were boarding.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did you finish work after that?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, I returned to work at noon.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What should we buy before the trip?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: A new bag and a phone charger.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: For the Hong Kong flight from the airport?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, and snacks for Mary.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Will Mary meet us after work?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: She will meet us near school at five.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Should we take your car?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, my car has more space for bags.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do we need to stop by the hospital?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Only briefly to pick up medicine.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: How far is your work from the airport?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: About twenty minutes by car.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: And from work to school?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Around ten minutes if traffic is light.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Can Mary carry this bag for you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, Mary said she can help.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do you still need to visit the hospital?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, before my Hong Kong flight.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That is a tight schedule.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It is, but I planned it after work.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Lets review the plan one more time.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Okay. First, school at eight.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Second, work meeting at ten.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Third, hospital visit at noon.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Fourth, pick up Mary and her bag.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Fifth, drive the car to the airport.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Sixth, check in for the Hong Kong flight.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Seventh, call work before boarding.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Great plan. Anything else?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, keep your bag and passport ready.

turns.speaker: B

turns.speaker: B

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Dont forget the hospital report.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Right, it is in my bag.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: And your airport ticket to Hong Kong?

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Also in my bag. I checked twice.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Great. Your car trip should be smooth.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Why are you late for work today?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: The road to school was blocked.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did you still reach the hospital on time?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, Mary waited with my bag at the gate.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That was helpful.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Very. Then we drove the car to the airport.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Was the airport crowded?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, many flights to Hong Kong were boarding.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Did you finish work after that?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, I returned to work at noon.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: What should we buy before the trip?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: A new bag and a phone charger.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: For the Hong Kong flight from the airport?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, and snacks for Mary.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Will Mary meet us after work?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: She will meet us near school at five.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Should we take your car?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, my car has more space for bags.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do we need to stop by the hospital?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Only briefly to pick up medicine.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: How far is your work from the airport?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: About twenty minutes by car.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: And from work to school?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Around ten minutes if traffic is light.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Can Mary carry this bag for you?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, Mary said she can help.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Do you still need to visit the hospital?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, before my Hong Kong flight.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: That is a tight schedule.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: It is, but I planned it after work.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Lets review the plan one more time.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Okay. First, school at eight.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Second, work meeting at ten.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Third, hospital visit at noon.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Fourth, pick up Mary and her bag.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Fifth, drive the car to the airport.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Sixth, check in for the Hong Kong flight.

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Seventh, call work before boarding.

turns.speaker: A
turns.english: Great plan. Anything else?

turns.speaker: B
turns.english: Yes, keep your bag and passport ready.
